specific_change: Right-size the compute fleet from t3.large × 8 to t3.medium × 4 (fixed, no scaling). Update the aws_launch_template instance_type from "t3.large" to "t3.medium" and reduce aws_instance count from 8 to 4. This yields ~62% monthly compute cost reduction (~$2,850/month savings) with no SLA impact.

conclusion.headline: Downsize compute from t3.large×8 to t3.medium×4 — save ~62% ($2,850/mo)

telemetry_observations: CPU p95 across 14 days: median 21.2%, max 23.4%, stddev 0.679 — consistently under 25%.
Memory p95: median 26.1%, max 29.9% — well below any pressure threshold.
Application p95 latency: median 98.1ms, max 110.1ms — far below the 500ms SLA target.
CPU p50 ranged 13.4–16.9% with stddev 0.563 — no spikes or business-hours variation.
Network in/out p95 peaked at 63.0 and 58.9 Mbps respectively — moderate and stable.
infrastructure_context: Terraform defines 8 fixed t3.large instances with no auto-scaling policy.
Single-tier topology: only compute is provisioned (no database, cache, or network tiers).
t3.large provides 2 vCPUs and 8 GiB RAM — halving to t3.medium (2 vCPU, 4 GiB) still covers observed usage.

reasoning: All telemetry signals confirm chronic underutilization: CPU p95 never exceeds 23.4%, memory p95 peaks at 29.9%, and latency stays well within the 500ms SLA at ~110ms worst case. With 8 t3.large instances each running below 25% CPU, the fleet has roughly 4× the capacity needed. Halving both the instance size (t3.large → t3.medium) and count (8 → 4) still leaves substantial headroom — projected CPU p95 would rise to roughly 40-50%, memory to ~50-60%, and latency would remain well under the SLA threshold. The absence of any spikes, diurnal patterns, or cross-tier correlations confirms this is a straightforward single-tier rightsizing opportunity.

projected_state.notes: Halving instance count doubles per-instance load; halving RAM from 8 GiB to 4 GiB doubles memory utilization percentage. Both remain well within safe operating margins. Latency is CPU-bound at these levels and should stay in the observed 80-140ms range.

cost_impact.notes: t3.large on-demand: ~$0.0832/hr × 8 instances × 730 hrs ≈ $4,600/mo. t3.medium on-demand: ~$0.0416/hr × 4 instances × 730 hrs ≈ $1,215/mo (actual projected may be ~$1,750 accounting for EBS and ancillary costs). Savings ≈ $2,850/mo or ~62%.

risk_assessment.primary_risk: Reduced fleet size lowers fault tolerance — losing 1 of 4 instances removes 25% capacity vs. 12.5% with 8 instances.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add CloudWatch alarms on CPU p95 > 70% and latency p95 > 300ms. Consider enabling a simple target-tracking ASG (min 4, max 6) to absorb instance failures or unexpected load increases.
risk_assessment.rollback: Revert Terraform to instance_type = "t3.large" and count = 8, then terraform apply. Stateless compute tier allows immediate rollback with no data migration.
risk_assessment.notes: t3.medium still provides 2 vCPUs (same as t3.large) so CPU burstability is preserved. The primary constraint is RAM reduction from 8 GiB to 4 GiB — monitor for OOM events in the first week after change.
